sections.title: Consumer Apps
sections.subtitle: A curated portfolio of consumer applications I've had the opportunity to work on across the B2C healthcare and technology industries. Click on any tile to learn more about each project.

cards.title: Set Your Working Hours
cards.problem: Our marketplace is supply-constrained because we ask therapists to elect their hours, and we're only showing 25% of the time slots our competitors are showing.
cards.metrics:
Primary: Doubled the amount of therapist availability per week
Secondary: $2M in ARR
cards.solution: Therapists set working hours, and we generate bookable time slots for clients. Anything outside of busy times from Google Calendar within working hours is considered a time slot.

cards.title: Simplified Availability Management
cards.problem: High-friction availability creation (12 clicks per timeslot in the EHR) artifically constraints supply and causes therapist frustration.
cards.metrics:
Primary: Added 2 hours of therapist availability per week
Secondary: $1M in ARR
cards.solution: Build a consolidated calendar that shows Google and EHR appointments in one place, decreasing the time to add new appointments by 75%.

cards.title: Therapist Daily Dashboard
cards.problem: Workflows spread across 4+ disparate systems; current daily email has stale data and <10% click rate despite >85% open rate.
cards.solution: Therapists can start their day with real-time insights served over a dashboard with caseload insights, one-click access to telehealth appointments, and actionable metrics.

cards.title: Proactive QA For Clinical Excellence
cards.problem: It takes 6 months for therapists to receive feedback from our QA team, because each therapist's case requires 2 hours of manual review.
cards.metrics: Quarterly review coverage for all therapists
cards.solution: Build admin QA tool with auditor UI, automated feedback dissemination, and duplicate note detection.

cards.title: Manage Your Active Clients
cards.problem: Therapists navigate 10+ disconnected systems to find basic client info; no accurate centralized caseload view in Therapist Portal.
cards.solution: Build comprehensive client list in Therapist Portal showing active clients, new vs. established, insurance, measures pending, and quick links.

cards.title: Multi-State Practice
cards.problem: Therapists licensed in multiple states can only practice in one, limiting supply in new markets and national payer attractiveness.
cards.metrics: Increase supply within our existing network
cards.solution: Configure our EHR to support multi-state availability, display, filtering, and claim processing for multi-state therapists.

cards.title: In-Person Appointments
cards.problem: 40%+ of therapy clients prefer in-person care, but we don't offer any in-person appointments.
cards.metrics: Increase supply within our existing network by 10%
cards.solution: Capture therapist in-person interest in our EHR, modify timeslots to support in-person appointments, and ensure appointments are billed as in-person.

sections.title: Business Integrations
sections.subtitle: A curated portfolio of business integrations I've had the opportunity to work on across the B2C healthcare and technology industries. Click on any tile to learn more about each project.

cards.title: Two-Way Google Calendar Sync
cards.problem: Therapists double-book with competitors because Octave appointments aren't visible on their Google Calendar.
cards.metrics:
Primary: Doubled the amount of therapist availability per week
Secondary: $2M in ARR
cards.solution: Write Octave appointments and holds back to therapist's Google Calendar as outbound events.

cards.title: Multi Google Calendar Support
cards.problem: 20% of therapists have multiple Google calendars but can only connect one, losing busy-block sync coverage.
cards.metrics:
Primary: Increase weekly availability by 10%
Secondary: $1M in ARR
cards.solution: Allow therapists to connect up to 10 total calendars to capture the full picture of their schedules.

cards.title: Build a Scalable Relational Therapist Database
cards.problem: **Therapist data lacks a single source of truth, fragmented across Airtable, Zoho, and Zapier with no defined schema — causing recurring sync failures, operational brittleness, and reliance on manual file uploads.
cards.metrics: Primary: Number of systems required to process a new therapist record
cards.solution: Build a relational SQL database as the internal source of truth for therapist data, replacing Airtable and piecemeal tooling.

cards.title: Self-Service Zendesk Support
cards.problem: 5× increase in support response times after therapist EHR transition; Slack-based support creates bottlenecks, tracking gaps, and operational burnout.
cards.metrics:
Primary: Therapist NPS
Secondary: Decrease support tickets by 20%
cards.solution: Implement in-app Zendesk live chat in Therapist Portal (reuse Client Portal model); deprecate Slack as therapist support channel.

cards.title: Support Messaging For Therapists
cards.problem: Therapists request support via Gmail and Slack, creating untrackable, unsecured requests with long turnaround times.
cards.solution: Therapists can message staff via the Portal, which creates a Zendesk message. This scales to our support volume of 2000+ tickets/month and allows staff to triage replies.

cards.title: Onboard Partner Therapist Network
cards.problem: We needed to onboard 10K+ therapists from a new partnership network, but our current onboarding process didn't scale to this volume.
cards.solution: Use exisitng Tellescope instance to build no-code workflows for therapists to complete enrollment, automate operational workflows (background checks, credentialing, etc.), and receive branded communications.

cards.title: Speed Up Time to Onboard
cards.problem: Onboarding spans 6+ systems, lacks staff visibility, and takes 47 days on average, causing 20% of therapists to offboard before their start date.
cards.metrics: Primary: Time to onboard (47 → <30 days target).
cards.solution: Bidirectional Tellescope ↔ therapist database sync; Tellescope as SSOT for therapist status/start date/bookable fields; retire Airtable as source of truth.

cards.title: Normalize and Enrich Insurance Directory Listings
cards.problem: We needed to generate outbound roster of 8K+ therapists as part of a new MSO partnership.
cards.metrics: Primary: Bookings with MSO partnership therapists from Partnerships directory. Initial volume is 10K therapists.
cards.solution: 1) Ingest MSO partnership CSV roster into therapist database; 2) Produce output file for Cigna directory.

cards.title: Move to a Biweekly Pay Schedule
cards.problem: Since we run a bimonthly payroll, therapists get paid on different days of the week. Our contracted therapists rely on consistent pay dates.
cards.metrics: Primary: % of therapists paid according to fixed schedule.
cards.solution: Roll out 16th/1st fixed pay schedule for all therapists; pay within 7 calendar days of pay period end.

cards.title: Streamline Payment Infrastructure For Faster Payouts
cards.problem: Each appointment required 6+ hours to sync between our systems before it could be processed for payment, requiring 15+ hrs of manual intervention per pay period.
cards.metrics: Primary: Time to sync (6 hrs -> 20 min). Secondary: Reduce ZD tickets by 50%.
cards.solution: Reduce time to sync by 95% by creating a new microservice to sync appointment data to the payment processing system.

cards.title: CRM to Streamline White-Glove Matching
cards.problem: To match a single client to a therapist, our white-glove care navigation team used 5+ spreadsheets to track client info and preferences. This process was tedious, taking 2 hours to match a client to a therapist. Our teams also couldn't track a client's progress through care after the initial match.
cards.solution: Implement Tellescope as a centralized CRM replacing the spreadsheets with integrated matching workflows, reducing Time to Match by 75%.
